Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /Users/ertugrulsahin/Codalio/demo-project-v4/booking-app/db/seeds.rb:
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	# Roles
     4	owner_role = Role.find_or_create_by!(slug: "owner") { |r| r.name = "Owner"; r.description = "Organization owner" }
     5	admin_role = Role.find_or_create_by!(slug: "admin") { |r| r.name = "Admin"; r.description = "Administrator" }
     6	
     7	# Organization
     8	org = Organization.find_or_create_by!(slug: "demo") do |o|
     9	  o.name = "Demo Org"
    10	  o.description = "Default demo organization"
    11	  o.is_active = true
    12	end
    13	
    14	# Org-level role permissions
    15	OrgRolePermission.find_or_create_by!(organization: org, role: owner_role) { |p| p.permissions = ["*"] }
    16	OrgRolePermission.find_or_create_by!(organization: org, role: admin_role) { |p| p.permissions = ["*"] }
    17	
    18	# Users
    19	alice = User.find_or_create_by!(email: "alice@example.com") do |u|
    20	  u.name = "Alice Johnson"
    21	  u.password = "password"
    22	end
    23	
    24	bob = User.find_or_create_by!(email: "bob@example.com") do |u|
    25	  u.name = "Bob Smith"
    26	  u.password = "password"
    27	end
    28	
    29	ur_alice = UserRole.find_or_create_by!(user: alice, organization: org, role: owner_role)
    30	ur_alice.update!(permissions: ["*"])
    31	
    32	ur_bob = UserRole.find_or_create_by!(user: bob, organization: org, role: admin_role)
    33	ur_bob.update!(permissions: ["*"])
    34	
    35	puts "Seeds complete!"
    36	puts "  alice@example.com / password  (owner)"
    37	puts "  bob@example.com   / password  (admin)"
    38
